description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage and a camouflage paint scheme in shades of brown, tan, and black. The helicopter features a large main rotor with four long, straight blades and a smaller tail rotor at the rear, both painted in a muted gray. The cockpit is located at the front, with a sloped, armored canopy and multiple angular windows for visibility. The landing gear consists of two robust wheels on each side, rather than traditional skids, suggesting a heavy-duty design suitable for rough terrain. The tail section is elongated and tapers to a vertical stabilizer, with a horizontal stabilizer and tail rotor mounted on the left side. The overall appearance is robust and functional, designed for tactical operations in urban or rugged environments.
description: A camouflaged, military-style helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, wheeled landing gear, and an angular armored cockpit. The design is robust and functional, suited for tactical operations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular cockpit, main rotor hub, and forward landing gear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, camouflage pattern, and main and tail rotors.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile, landing gear, and tail structure.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored gray with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and appear robust.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small tail rotor with four blades, mounted vertically on the left side of the tail. The blades are gray and appear to be made of composite material, with no visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: This helicopter uses wheeled landing gear instead of skids. The gear consists of two main wheels on each side, with visible struts and supports, likely made of steel and rubber, colored black and metallic.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows at the front, arranged in a sloped, armored canopy. The windows are medium-sized, tinted, and framed with dark material, providing wide visibility.
